//: Type-checking config for a JS-only project. We don't compile anything.
tsc runs as a linter (--noEmit) and reads JSDoc annotations to catch
typos, wrong shapes, and undeclared variables across our 9.5k LoC of
vanilla JS. Adding /// <reference> or @type comments in source files
is enough to opt code into checking — no .ts files, no transpile step.
Run via:  npx -y -p typescript tsc --noEmit  (also wired into CI)
Strictness is dialed down to a level we can hit today; tighten over
time as JSDoc coverage grows. The whole point is to flag obvious bugs
before users see them, not to win a type-purity contest.
//include: Type-check the numeric core, the storage layer, and the parser — the
parts where a wrong type means a wrong number for a broker or lost
data. UI screens are not yet JSDocced enough for tsc to be useful (the
narrow inference from dom.js helpers produces noise, not signal).
Adding screens incrementally is fine; do it by JSDoccing src/ui/dom.js
first so the inference is broad enough to type-check downstream callers.
tests/ and tools/ live in node-land and would need @types/node to type-
check, which means a package.json — explicitly avoided by this project.
They're small (<500 LoC) and exercise themselves at runtime via CI.
